ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
erose: adjective. Irregularly notched, eaten away, as though bitten.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
ness: noun. A promontory; a cape or headland. (Frequently used as a suffix in placenames.)
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
opener: noun. A person who opens something.
opes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ope
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
oses: noun. The various sugars ending in -ose, such as sucrose, lactose, glucose, and fructose.
pee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taje.
peen: noun. The (often spherical) end of the head of a hammer opposite the main hammering end.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
pen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Penesak.
peon: noun. A lowly person; a peasant or serf; a labourer who is obliged to do menial work.
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
perse: noun. A very dark (almost black) purple or blue-gray colour.
person: noun. An individual who has been granted personhood; usually a human being.
persons: noun. plural of person, used to refer to them individually, rather than as a group; contrast people
pes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranian Persian.
peso: noun. A former unit of currency in Spain and Spain's colonies, worth 8 reales; the Spanish dollar.
pone: noun. A writ in law used by the superior courts to remove cases from inferior courts.
pons: noun. A bridge-like tissue connecting two parts of an organ.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
pose: noun. Common cold, head cold; catarrh.
poser: noun. A particularly difficult question or puzzle.
posse: noun. A group or company of people, originally especially one having hostile intent; a throng, a crowd.
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
preen: noun. A forked tool used by clothiers for dressing cloth.
prees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pree
press: noun. An instance of applying pressure; an instance of pressing.
pro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old Occitan.
prose: noun. Language, particularly written language, not intended as poetry.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reopens: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of reopen
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
repose: verb. To lay (someone, or part of their body) down to rest.
response: noun. An answer or reply, or something in the nature of an answer or reply.
roe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ronji.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seep: verb. To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
sees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of see
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
sense: noun. Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the physical world: for humans sight, smell, hearing, touch, taste.
sensor: noun. A device or organ that detects certain external stimuli and responds in a distinctive manner.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
sneer: verb. To raise a corner of the upper lip slightly, especially in scorn.
sneers: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sneer
snore: verb. To breathe during sleep with harsh, snorting noises caused by vibration of the soft palate.
snores: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of snore
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
sop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Songe.
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
sorns: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sorn
sos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seenku.
spore: noun. A reproductive particle, usually a single cell, released by a fungus, alga, or plant that may germinate into another.
